---
title: "Quarto Exercises Extension Example"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: cosmo
      dark: darkly
filters:
  - quarto-exercises
quarto-exercises:
  instant: false
  reveal: true
  lock: false
  reset: true
  shuffle: false
  show-answers: false
---

This document shows how to use the `quarto-exercises` extension to write interactive practice exercises in Quarto HTML pages.

## Multiple Choice / Multiple Answer

### Minimal Single-Choice

::: {.exercise}
Who carried the One Ring out of the Shire?

::: {.answer correct=true}
Frodo Baggins
:::

::: {.answer}
Boromir
:::
:::

### Multiple-Correct with Custom Keys & Shuffling

::: {.exercise shuffle=true}
Which members of the Fellowship are hobbits? Select all that apply.

::: {.answer correct=true key="frodo"}
Frodo Baggins
:::

::: {.answer correct=true key="sam"}
Samwise Gamgee
:::

::: {.answer key="legolas"}
Legolas Greenleaf
:::

::: {.answer correct=true key="merry"}
Meriadoc Brandybuck
:::

::: {.hint}
There are four hobbits in the Fellowship. One of them is Peregrin Took (Pippin), who is not listed here.
:::
:::

### Per-Answer Feedback & Question Explanation

::: {.exercise}
What is the name of the wizard who guides the Fellowship?

::: {.answer correct=true}
Gandalf

::: {.feedback}
Correct! Specifically Gandalf the Grey, who later becomes Gandalf the White.
:::
:::

::: {.answer}
Saruman

::: {.feedback}
Not quite. Saruman resides in Isengard and betrays the Fellowship.
:::
:::

::: {.explanation}
Gandalf is one of the Istari (wizards) sent to Middle-earth to oppose Sauron.
:::
:::

### Code Blocks in Choices

::: {.exercise}
What does the following Python statement print?

```python
print([x * 2 for x in range(3)])
```

::: {.answer}
`[1, 2, 3]`
:::

::: {.answer correct=true}
`[0, 2, 4]`

::: {.feedback}
Correct! `range(3)` produces `0, 1, 2`, and multiplying each by 2 yields `0, 2, 4`.
:::
:::
:::


## Fill-in-the-blank

### Basic Blank

The wizard who guides the Fellowship is [`Gandalf`]{.blank answer="Gandalf"}.

### Multiple Accepted Answers (Case-insensitive)

The Ringbearer is [`Frodo`]{.blank answers="Frodo|Frodo Baggins" ignore-case=true}.

### Regex Matching

The full title of the first volume of Lord of the Rings is [`The Fellowship of the Ring`]{.blank answer="^The\s+Fellowship\s+of\s+the\s+Ring$" match="regex" ignore-case=true}.


## Inline Choice / Dropdown Cloze

### Standalone Dropdowns

The One Ring was forged in [Mordor|Gondor|Rohan]{.choose answer="Mordor"}.

### Nested in a Parent Exercise

Grouping inline choices and blanks under a parent `.exercise` block combines their Check and Reset controls:

::: {.exercise}
The hobbits are saved at the Prancing Pony by [Aragorn|Boromir|Legolas|Gimli]{.choose answer="Aragorn"}, who is also known as [Strider]{.blank answer="Strider"}.

::: {.hint}
What does Sam call him?
:::
:::

## Code Cloze

Blanks and dropdowns can live directly inside code blocks. Syntax highlighting is fully preserved.

A standalone `.code-cloze` block gets its own Check and Reset buttons:

```{.code-cloze lang="r"}
x <- {{choose answer="c" options="c|list|data.frame"}}(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
total <- {{blank answer="sum"}}(x)
cat("Total:", total, "\n")
```

Wrapping a `.code-cloze` block in an `.exercise` div gives it the same Check and Reset controls as any other exercise:

::: {.exercise}
```{.code-cloze lang="python"}
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
total = {{choose answer="sum" options="sum|max|min|len"}}(numbers)
print({{blank answer="total"}})
```
:::

::: {.exercise}
```{.code-cloze lang="r"}
x <- 1:10
mean <- {{choose answer="mean" options="mean|median|sum|length"}}(x)
cat({{blank answer="mean"}}, "\n")
```
:::
